## Gomphidae

[â† Back to Gomphidae in Family Index](/field-notes/insects/odonata/anistoptera/family#gomphidae)
{: style="text-align: center;"}

**Gomphidae** (clubtails) are typically medium-sized, stocky dragonflies distinguished by widely separated eyes and, in many species, an expanded terminal abdomen (the â€œclubâ€) in males. They are often found along rivers and streams and frequently perch on sandy banks or bare ground.

### Field marks
- Eyes widely separated on the head
- Frequently a widened terminal abdomen in males
- Often perch on ground, sandy banks, or low vegetation

### Habitat
Common along flowing waterâ€”rivers and streamsâ€”and adjacent open banks or gravel bars.

### Representative species
- Common Clubtail (*Gomphus* and related genera)
